questions.question: Which of the following is the main objective of the Right to Information Act?
questions.option_1: To prevent public access to government records
questions.option_2: To provide private data to media houses
questions.option_3: To ensure secrecy in public administration
questions.solution:
Objectives of RTI Act

The Right to Information (RTI) Act, 2005 was enacted to promote transparency and accountability in the working of every public authority. It empowers citizens to secure access to information under the control of public authorities, thereby containing corruption and making democracy work for the people in a real sense.

questions.question: As of July 2025, how many Indian States have a bicameral legislature (i.e. both, a Legislative Assembly and a Legislative Council)?
questions.solution:
Bicameral Legislatures in India

As of 2025, six Indian states have a bicameral legislature (consisting of both a Legislative Assembly and a Legislative Council): Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, and Uttar Pradesh. Most other states have a unicameral system with only an Assembly.

questions.question: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) holders are:
questions.option_1: Dual citizens of India and another country
questions.option_2: Persons entitled to hold constitutional posts
questions.solution:
Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)

OCI holders are foreign nationals (holding a foreign passport) who are granted a multi-purpose, multi-entry, lifelong visa for visiting India. They enjoy special privileges like parity with NRIs in economic and educational fields, but they cannot vote, hold constitutional posts, or own agricultural land.

questions.question: Which Amendment extended the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the legislatures for another 10 years till 2030?
questions.solution:
104th Amendment Act

The 104th Amendment Act (2020) extended the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies for another 10 years, until 25 January 2030. Notably, this same amendment discontinued the reservation of seats for the Anglo-Indian community.

questions.question: In Indian State governments, to whom is the State Council of Ministers collectively responsible?
questions.option_1: Chief Justice of the concerned State
questions.solution:
Collective Responsibility

In a parliamentary form of government at the state level, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the State Legislative Assembly. This means the ministry stays in power only as long as it enjoys the confidence (majority support) of the lower house.

questions.question: The Eleventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution relates to which of the following?
questions.solution:
Eleventh Schedule: Panchayats

Added by the 73rd Amendment Act of 1992, the Eleventh Schedule contains 29 functional items placed within the purview of Panchayats. It covers subjects like agriculture, land improvement, minor irrigation, and rural housing.

questions.question: What key principle did the Supreme Court emphasise in the 2024 Property Owners Association v State of Maharashtra case?
questions.option_1: Only scarce or vital resources justify state takeover.
questions.option_2: Public trust doctrine applies to all private assets.
questions.option_3: State can acquire all private properties at will.
questions.option_4: Government power overrides individual property rights.
questions.solution:
Property Owners Association Case (2024)

In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court clarified that the government cannot claim 'all' private property as 'community resources' under Article 39(b). It emphasized that only scarce or vital resources of a private nature may be considered 'material resources of the community' to justify a state takeover for the common good.

questions.question: According to Article 79 of the Indian Constitution, the Parliament of India consists of:
questions.option_1: President, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha
questions.option_2: President, Vice President, and Prime Minister
questions.option_3: Prime Minister, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha
questions.option_4: only Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
questions.solution:
Composition of Parliament

According to Article 79, the Parliament of the Union consists of the President and two Houses to be known respectively as the Council of States (Rajya Sabha) and the House of the People (Lok Sabha).

questions.question: Special provisions for certain states are covered under which Part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution:
Special Provisions (Part XXI)

Part XXI of the Constitution contains Temporary, Transitional, and Special Provisions. This includes Articles 371 to 371-J, which provide special provisions for states like Maharashtra, Gujarat, Nagaland, Assam, Manipur, Andhra Pradesh, Sikkim, Mizoram, Arunachal Pradesh, Goa, and Karnataka.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha?
questions.solution:
Article 330: SC/ST in Lok Sabha

Article 330 provides for the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha. The number of seats reserved is based on their proportion to the total population of the State or Union Territory.

questions.question: What is the term period of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India?
questions.solution:
Tenure of the CAG

The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India holds office for a term of 6 years or until the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier. The CAG can be removed from office only in the same manner and on the same grounds as a Judge of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which act gave statutory status to the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC)?
questions.solution:
CVC Act, 2003

While the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) was originally set up by a government resolution in 1964, it was granted statutory status by the Central Vigilance Commission Act, 2003, following a Supreme Court direction in the Vineet Narain case.

questions.question: Which of the following central services holds the highest prestige, pay and emoluments among its peers?
questions.solution:
Prestige of Indian Foreign Service

Among the Central Services (Group A), the Indian Foreign Service (IFS) is often considered to hold the highest prestige and unique emoluments, such as the Foreign Allowance, which varies based on the cost of living in the country of posting, making it highly sought after.

questions.question: The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution is related to the:
questions.option_4: distribution of seats in the Lok Sabha
questions.solution:
Tenth Schedule: Anti-Defection

The Tenth Schedule, popularly known as the Anti-Defection Law, was added by the 52nd Amendment Act in 1985. It provides for the disqualification of members of Parliament and State Legislatures on the grounds of defection from one political party to another.

questions.question: What major change was made by the 86ᵗʰ Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002?
questions.option_3: Declared education as a Fundamental Right
questions.solution:
86th Amendment: Right to Education

The 86th Amendment Act (2002) made elementary education a Fundamental Right by inserting Article 21A. It mandates the State to provide free and compulsory education to all children between the ages of 6 and 14 years.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an instrument of parliamentary control over the executive in India?
questions.option_2: No-confidence motion against the Council of Ministers
questions.option_3: Question Hour in the Lok Sabha
questions.option_4: Approval of government bills (laws) by the Parliament
questions.solution:
Parliamentary vs. Judicial Control

Judicial Review is a power of the courts (Judiciary) to check the executive. In contrast, Question Hour, No-Confidence Motions, and Approval of Bills are direct instruments of Parliamentary control used by the legislature to hold the executive accountable.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution was amended by the 106ᵗʰ Constitutional Amendment Act to provide for women’s reservation in the Legislative Assembly of Delhi?
questions.solution:
106th Amendment and Delhi

The 106th Constitutional Amendment Act (Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam) amended Article 239AA to ensure that the one-third reservation for women also applies to the Legislative Assembly of the National Capital Territory of Delhi.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution provides special provisions for the State of Nagaland?
questions.solution:
Special Provisions for Nagaland

Article 371A provides special provisions for Nagaland. It protects Naga religious and social practices, customary law, and ownership/transfer of land from Acts of Parliament unless the Nagaland Legislative Assembly decides otherwise.

questions.question: The President of India is also the Supreme Commander of which force in India?
questions.solution:
President as Supreme Commander

Under Article 53, the executive power of the Union is vested in the President, who is also the Supreme Commander of the Defence Forces (Army, Navy, and Air Force) of India.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes a key function of Subordinate Courts in India?
questions.option_1: Resolve civil and criminal disputes at the district level
questions.solution:
Function of Subordinate Courts

The Subordinate Courts (District and lower courts) function under the administrative control of the High Court. Their key role is to resolve civil and criminal disputes at the district level and below, serving as the first point of contact for most litigants.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right protects citizens from being forced into labour or human trafficking?
questions.solution:
Right against Exploitation

Articles 23 and 24 constitute the Right against Exploitation. Article 23 specifically prohibits human trafficking, 'begar' (forced labour), and other similar forms of forced labour, making such acts punishable by law.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution states, 'no person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law'?
questions.solution:
Article 300A: Right to Property

After being removed as a Fundamental Right by the 44th Amendment, the Right to Property was shifted to Article 300A as a Constitutional/Legal right. It states that "no person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law."

questions.question: Under the Fifth Schedule, which primary authority is granted to the Governor of a State regarding Scheduled Areas?
questions.option_1: Power to regulate land, forests and social customs in Scheduled Areas
questions.option_2: Power to dissolve the State Legislative Assembly
questions.option_3: Authority to appoint Members of Parliament from Scheduled Areas
questions.option_4: Power to amend the Constitution regarding Schedule amendments
questions.solution:
Governor's Authority in Scheduled Areas

Under the Fifth Schedule, the Governor has the power to direct that a particular Act of Parliament or State Legislature does not apply to a Scheduled Area. They can also make regulations for the peace and good government of such areas, particularly regarding land and social customs.

questions.question: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution is commonly cited as forming the constitutional basis for Judicial Review?
questions.solution:
Article 13: Basis of Judicial Review

Article 13 is the bedrock of Judicial Review in India. It declares that all laws that are inconsistent with or in derogation of the Fundamental Rights shall be void. This gives the High Courts and Supreme Court the power to strike down unconstitutional legislation.

questions.question: What is the main feature of the 106ᵗʰ Constitutional Amendment Act, 2023?
questions.option_2: Reservation of one-third seats for women in legislatures
questions.option_3: Provision of legal status to Right to Property
questions.solution:
Women’s Reservation Bill (2023)

The 106th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2023, provides for the reservation of one-third of all seats for women in the Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies, and the Delhi Legislative Assembly. This reservation will come into effect after the first census and delimitation exercise following the Act's commencement.
